subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Tiles
- **84 Land tiles** — Base tile set for roads, cities, monasteries, and fields.
- **12 River tiles** — Included in the 84; used with supplementary river rules.
- **1 Start tile** — Dark-backed start tile used to begin the map.
- **Tile backs** — Start tile + River tiles have dark backs; other tiles have regular backs.

#### Scoring & Markers
- **1 scoreboard** — Used to track points during play.

#### Meeples & Extras
- **40 regular meeples** — 8 each in yellow, red, green, blue, and black.
- **5 abbots** — 1 abbot in each player color.
- **First-game note** — Set abbots aside if teaching base rules only.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Start tile** — Place the dark-backed start tile face up in the center.
2. **Tile stacks** — Shuffle remaining playable tiles and make face-down stacks within reach.
3. **Scoreboard** — Place scoreboard beside the play area where all players can see it.
4. **Table space** — Leave open room in all directions for map growth.
5. **Intro setup** — Keep River tiles and abbots out unless using supplementary rules.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Color choice** — Each player picks one color.
- **Personal supply** — Take 7 regular meeples of that color into your supply.
- **Score marker** — Place your 8th regular meeple on space 0 of the scoreboard.
- **Unused colors** — Return unused color pieces to the box.
- **Abbots in intro game** — Return abbots to the box for base teaching games.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — Choose a starting player (youngest is suggested in the rules).
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.
- **Turn flow** — Each turn follows the same 3-step sequence:
1. **Place tile** — Draw and place 1 tile.
2. **Place meeple (optional)** — Place 1 meeple on the tile you just placed, if legal.
3. **Score** — Score any features completed by that placement.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Draw & place** — Draw exactly 1 land tile and place it legally.
2. **Meeple step (optional)** — Place 1 meeple from supply on the just-placed tile, if legal.
3. **Score completions** — Score every feature completed by that tile placement.
4. **Return meeples** — Return scored meeples to their owners’ supplies.
5. **Pass turn** — Next player clockwise takes their turn.

- **Scoring timing** — Completed features score immediately at end of the active player’s turn.
- **Who can score** — Any player with qualifying meeples scores, not only the active player.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Tile Placement
- **Legal match** — Adjacent edges must continue correctly (road-road, city-city, field-field).
- **No legal spot** — If placement is impossible, return tile to box and draw a new one.

#### Meeple Placement
- **Optional placement** — You may place 1 meeple only on the tile you just placed.
- **Occupancy rule** — You may not place into a connected feature that already has any meeple.
- **Role by location** — Road = highwayman, city = knight, monastery = monk, field = farmer.

#### Road Scoring
- **Completion** — Road closes at both ends (city, village, monastery, or loops to itself).
- **Points** — Score 1 point per tile in that completed road.
- **Meeple return** — Return meeple(s) from the scored road.

#### City Scoring (During Game)
- **Completion** — City is fully enclosed by walls with no gaps.
- **Tile value** — Score 2 points per city tile.
- **Coat of arms value** — Score +2 points per coat of arms.
- **Meeple return** — Return meeple(s) after scoring.

#### Monastery Scoring (During Game)
- **Completion** — Monastery tile is surrounded on all 8 adjacent spaces.
- **Points** — Score 1 point per surrounding tile + 1 for the monastery tile (max 9).
- **Meeple return** — Return the monk after scoring.

#### Majority & Shared Features
- **Merged features** — Separate features can connect later and hold multiple players’ meeples.
- **Majority wins** — Player with most meeples scores full points.
- **Tie for most** — All tied players score full points.
- **Minority result** — Players with fewer meeples score 0.

#### Score Tracking
- **Track movement** — Move your scoring marker whenever you score.
- **50+ reminder** — Lay marker down after passing 50 points, as noted in rules.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Feature Merging
- **Core tactic** — You cannot place directly into an occupied connected feature.
- **Workaround** — Place into a separate feature, then connect it later.
- **Why it matters** — Merges create majority contests and swing scoring.

#### Road Merge Example
- **Setup** — Two separate roads each have one highwayman.
- **Merge** — A later tile connects those roads.
- **Result** — If tied for most meeples, both players score full road points.

#### City Takeover Example
- **Setup** — Opponent has a knight in one city section.
- **Counterplay** — You place a knight in a separate city section.
- **Merge** — A later tile connects sections into one city.
- **Result** — If you now have majority knights, only you score that city (unless tied).

#### Farmers & Fields
- **Rule status** — Final scoring includes fields at 3 points per adjacent completed city.
- **Teaching advice** — Add farmers/fields after players learn roads, cities, monasteries, and majority basics.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **End trigger** — Game ends immediately after the turn when the last tile is placed.

#### Final Scoring (Meeples Still on Map)
- **Incomplete road** — 1 point per tile.
- **Incomplete city** — 1 point per tile + 1 point per coat of arms.
- **Incomplete monastery** — 1 point for monastery tile + 1 point per adjacent surrounding tile.
- **Field scoring** — 3 points per adjacent completed city.

#### Majority Rules in Final Scoring
- **Most meeples** — Player with most meeples in a feature scores full points.
- **Tie for most** — All tied players score full points.

#### All Point Sources
- **Completed roads (during play)** — 1 point per road tile.
- **Completed cities (during play)** — 2 points per city tile + 2 per coat of arms.
- **Completed monasteries (during play)** — Up to 9 points each.
- **Incomplete roads (final)** — 1 point per tile.
- **Incomplete cities (final)** — 1 point per tile + 1 per coat of arms.
- **Incomplete monasteries (final)** — 1 + surrounding adjacent tiles.
- **Fields (final)** — 3 points per adjacent completed city.

- **Winner** — Highest total points wins.
- **Tie** — Tied highest scores share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Score-and-recycle early** — Prioritize short roads and closeable small cities to recover meeples quickly.
- **Keep reserves** — Maintain 2-3 meeples in supply so you can react to good tile draws.
- **Place for two goals** — Choose tiles that improve your feature and make opponent closures harder.
- **Monastery timing** — Place monasteries where nearby spaces are likely to fill soon.
- **Arms-city planning** — Contest coat-of-arms cities early; they are high-value and often game-deciding.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Meeple lock-up** — Committing too many meeples to long unfinished features leaves you action-starved.
- **No majority defense** — Assuming a feature is safe invites merge takeovers and major point swings.
- **Overgrown cities** — Expanding cities without closure paths often converts 2-point tiles into weaker final scoring.
- **Helping opponents accidentally** — Closing enemy features on your turn can gift instant points and meeple returns.
- **Late-game blind spot** — Ignoring tile depletion and final-scoring values causes missed conversions and denial plays.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Safe points vs big gambles** — Decide between guaranteed quick closures and larger but uncertain projects.
- **Contest vs isolation** — Enter shared features for majority fights or build isolated features for reliable solo scoring.
- **Self-advance vs denial** — Evaluate whether each tile is better used to boost your score line or block an opponent’s finish.

metadata.notes: Primary source: provided Carcassonne rulebook text in prompt; component counts and scoring values copied from that source scope.
